title: pterotropia kavaiensis
intro: Pterotropia kavaiensis, also known as the Hawaiian stinging tree or ʻōhā wai, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Hawaii, characterized by its distinctive, stinging hairs that contain an irritant compound called kalopanaxin. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the kalopanaxin in its stinging hairs. Pterotropia kavaiensis is believed to offer several health benefits, including pain relief, wound healing, and protection against infection. In herbal preparations, the plant's stinging hairs can be used to create a topical cream or poultice, although proper handling and preparation are essential to avoid irritation.
